titleStmt.title: SII 34.220: original edition by Y. Subbarayalu
No. 220. Ammankurichchi, Tirumayam Taluk, Pudukkottai District. On a slab set up in the maṇḍapa in front of the Sundarēśvara temple.

summary.p: Nayaka, Vīra-Pūchchaya-nāyaka, Śaka 1613, Prajōtpatti, Chittirai 2 = A.D. 1691.

The first part (line 5—12) of the record gives a eulogy of the family of the donor chief Vīra-Pūchchaya-Nayakkar, who was the son of Ōvaḷa-Pūchchaya-nāyakkar, who himself was a brother of Tirumalai-Pūchchaya-nāyakkar. He remitted all taxes on the peasants, merchants and other citizens (kuḍi-paḍai) living in the tiruviḷaiyāṭṭam village Namaṉakuṟichchi and its six hamlets in favour of the temple of Chokkar in Namaṉakuṟichchi. However the existing tax-free categories like dēvadāya, brahmadāya, maḍappuṟam, and umbiḷigai-jīvitam grants were exempted from the present grant. It is said the order will apply to the immigrant kuḍis from outside too. The existing rates of fines were also reduced.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī cālivākaṉa cakāttam 1000 6 100 10 3
Itaṉmēṟ cellāniṉṟa piṟacōṟpa
ti varuṣam cittirai mātam 2 § cupavāramum cu
panaṭcettiramum cupayōka cupaka
raṇamum peṟṟa cupatiṉattil cuñ
cai Antampar kaṇṭaṉ cāṭi
kkāṟar kaṇṭaṉ cammaṭṭiṉārāyaṇa
ṉ Urikōla curatāṇaṉ pañcavaṉṉappā
vāṭairāyaṉ rāyarākutta miṇṭaṉ rācātirā
caṉ rāca kempīraṉ rācamāttāṇṭaṉ racci maṇi
maruṅkaiyampatiyāṉ maruvalarkēcari vēḷa malaiyiṉāṉ
pālāṟuṭaiyāṉ rācavēciyar pucaṅ
kaṉ Orucol vācakaṉāṉa śrītirumalaippūc
ceya nāyakkar cakōtarar Ovaḷappūccayanā
yakkar kumāra vīrappūcceya nāyakkar namaṉa
kuṟicci cokkar tiruviḷaiyāṭṭamāṉa namaṉa
kuṟicci Il Itiṉuṭaṉ cēnta cāṅkaṉ maṉayaṭi mātaṉ
paṭṭi nākamaṅkalam tiraṇipaṭṭi paḷḷakkō
ṭaṅkipaṭṭi maṭattuppaṭṭi mutalāṉa kirāmattilu
ṇṭāṉa kuṭiyāṉa pērkaḷil ceṭṭi vaṟtakar
kōvil tāṉattār talattār Itil kuṭiyirukki
ṟa pirāmaṇar paṇṭāraṅkaḷ kōyil maṇṭapak
kottār mutalākiya pērukaḷukku cilācātaṉa
ppaṭṭaiyamāka kallu veṭṭikkuṭutta paṭi Inta
civastatalam nammuṭa vaṅkiṣattilē piṟa tiṭṭai
paṇṇiṉa kulāpimāṉamāṉa taṉmamāṉa paṭiyiṉā
lē Inta vakaiyilē Oru paṇam Orukācākilum
nāmāciyāmal caṟuvamāṉiyamāka naṭappikkiṟōmeṉ
ṟu cokka nātacuvāmi caṉṉatiyilē maṉappūṟuvamā
kaccollik kallu veṭṭikkuṭutta paṭiyiṉālē In
takkirāmaṅkaḷilē Irukkiṟa kuṭipaṭaiyuluṇṭākiya
pakuti ceṭṭivaṟkattār poṟuppu tēvatāyam piṟumatāyam
māṉiyam maṭappuṟam Umpiḷikai cīvitam Inta
vakaiyil Aṟuvākiṟa muṟaivari kaṭamai kācum
ñāyamāṉapaṭikku Aṟuviṭṭu Antappaṇamum Iti
l cāntapēr ceyyāta kuṟṟam kuṟaiceytavar
ḷiṭattilumBūchayaThis is the name of the Chief and written in Telugu characters. It seems to be placed in a symbolic way in the centre of the inscription.vāṅkiṟa Aparā
tam paṉṉiraṇṭu Iruvattu
nālu paṇamē Allāmal Atiyam tēvai
yillai Anta vicēṣavakai Uḷpukkaṇ
ṭa tellāṅ kōvilukkē kuṭuttuc cu
vāmi kāriyam Aṉukūlamāka naṭakkumpaṭikkum
Itilc cārnta caṉaṅkaḷiṭattil teṇṭatō
ṣa parumpiṭi ñāya tappu naṭappikkiṟatillai yeṉṟu
niyamiccu muttaḻakiri nāyakkar vicārippilum kuḻantai
vēlapiḷḷai maṇiyattil Avar coṟpaṭikkuc cilā
likitamāka kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭōm Intappaṭikku nām
pūṟaṇa Apimāṉamāka Apimāṉittu
c cantirātittar Uḷḷa varaikkum
Oruvar Orukācu Orupaṇamāṉālum
Ācikkaillaiyeṉṟu caṟuvamāṉiya
mākak kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭuk kalveṭṭik koṭu
ttōm Intappaṭikku namaṉ kuṟicci
yil cērnta grāmaṅkaḷilirukkum kuṭi
yāṉa pērukkum piṟac cīrmaiyil varukiṟa
kuṭikku talattār tāṉam palarukkum cilācātaṉa
paṭi naṭappikkiṟatu Uṭccīrmaikkuṭikkuṭaṉ
piṭikkai tēvaiyillaiyeṉṟu cilā
cātaṉam paṇṇikkoṭuttōm Intapaṭi
kku ceṭṭi vattakar kuṭiyāṉavar palarum nammai
nampikkuṭivantu cukattilē yirukkavum Intaṉmam
ttukku Iṭaiyūṟu paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅkai
kkaraiyilē kapilai karāvaiyu pirāma
ṇa pativiṟataiyaḷaiyu mātāpitāvai
yu kuruvaiyum koṉṟa tōṣattilē
pōvāṉ § Ippaṭikku cokkar tuṇai §
Digital edition of SII 34.220 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
